India Business.title: RBI holds repo rate at 6.5% for third consecutive meeting
India Business.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee unanimously voted to maintain the repo rate at 6.5%, citing persistent inflationary pressures above the 4% target. Governor Shaktikanta Das signaled a cautious stance, noting global uncertainty from US Fed policy as a key factor.

India Business.title: Tata Motors EV sales cross 10,000 units in May, up 38% YoY
India Business.summary: Tata Motors reported record electric vehicle sales in May, driven by strong demand for the Nexon EV and Punch EV in Tier-1 and Tier-2 cities. The company now commands over 62% of India's EV passenger vehicle market, well ahead of nearest competitor MG Motor.

Startups & VC.title: Zepto raises $350M Series F at $5B valuation ahead of IPO
Startups & VC.summary: Quick-commerce startup Zepto closed a $350 million funding round led by General Atlantic and StepStone, pushing its valuation to $5 billion. The funds will accelerate dark store expansion in 20 new cities and build out its private label grocery business before a planned 2026 IPO.

Government & Policy.title: Finance Ministry proposes PLI scheme extension for electronics manufacturing
Government & Policy.summary: The Ministry of Finance has circulated a proposal to extend the Production-Linked Incentive scheme for electronics manufacturing by three years, covering smartphones, laptops, and components. The move aims to attract an additional $8 billion in FDI from companies diversifying supply chains out of China.

Global Markets.title: Fed signals one rate cut in 2026 as inflation edges toward 2.5% target
Global Markets.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated the central bank is prepared to cut rates once in 2026 if inflation continues trending toward the 2.5% mark. Markets responded positively, with the S&P 500 rising 0.8% on the guidance.

US & Global Economy.title: IMF raises global growth forecast to 3.4% for 2026
US & Global Economy.summary: The International Monetary Fund upgraded its global growth projection to 3.4% for 2026, up from its January estimate of 3.1%, citing stronger-than-expected performance in the US, India, and Southeast Asia. The Fund warned that escalating trade tensions remain significant downside factors.
